---
title: "Priming analysis: SBE"
author: "Sean Trott"
date: "2-23-2026"
output:
  html_document:
    keep_md: yes
    toc: yes
    toc_float: yes
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(dpi = 300, fig.format = "png")
```


# Setup

Load libraries

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(broom.mixed)
library(lmerTest)
library(ggridges)
```


# Load data

```{r}
# setwd("/Users/seantrott/Dropbox/UCSD/Research/Ambiguity/SSD/llm_sbe/src/analysis")

### First, load all pythia models
directory_path <- "../../data/processed/leinenger/"
csv_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = "*.csv", full.names = TRUE)
csv_list <- csv_files %>%
  map(~ read_csv(.))
df_pythia_models <- bind_rows(csv_list) %>%
  mutate(model_shorthand = sub("^EleutherAI/", "", model)) 
nrow(df_pythia_models)
table(df_pythia_models$model_shorthand)
```


# Analysis

## Visualization over time

```{r leinenger-dev, fig.width=12, fig.height=7, out.width="100%", dpi=300}


cor.test(df_pythia_models$target_span_surprisal_mean, 
         df_pythia_models$target_token_surprisal)

cor.test(df_pythia_models$target_span_surprisal_sum, 
         df_pythia_models$target_token_surprisal)

df_pythia_models %>%
  filter(repeated == "no") %>%
  group_by(model_shorthand, condition, checkpoint, n_params) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_surprisal = mean(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_surprisal = sd(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = checkpoint,
             y = mean_surprisal,
             color = condition)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1000, linetype = "dotted", size = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_surprisal - se_surprisal,
                  ymax = mean_surprisal + se_surprisal,
                  fill = condition),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  scale_x_log10(
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("dominant" = "#4E79A7", "subordinate" = "#E15759")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dominant" = "#4E79A7", "subordinate" = "#E15759")) +
  labs(x = "Checkpoint (log scale)",
       y = "Target token surprisal",
       color = "Condition",
       fill = "Condition") +
  theme_minimal() +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(model_shorthand, n_params), ncol = 4) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "vertical",          # stack the shape + color legends
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),   # shrink legend text
        legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm")) 



df_pythia_models %>%
  group_by(model_shorthand, condition, repeated, checkpoint, n_params) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_surprisal = mean(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_surprisal = sd(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = checkpoint,
             y = mean_surprisal,
             color = condition,
             linetype = repeated)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1000, linetype = "dotted", size = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_surprisal - se_surprisal,
                  ymax = mean_surprisal + se_surprisal,
                  fill = condition),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  scale_x_log10(
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("dominant" = "#4E79A7", "subordinate" = "#E15759")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("dominant" = "#4E79A7", "subordinate" = "#E15759")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("yes" = "solid", "no" = "dashed")) +
  labs(x = "Checkpoint (log scale)",
       y = "Target token surprisal",
       color = "Condition",
       fill = "Condition",
       linetype = "Repeated") +
  theme_minimal() +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(model_shorthand, n_params), ncol = 4) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "vertical",          # stack the shape + color legends
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),   # shrink legend text
        legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm")) 




```


## Final checkpoint

Plot effect at final level:

```{r leinenger_final_checkpoint}
### analysis of interaction at final checkpoint


df_pythia_models %>%
  filter(checkpoint == 143000) %>%
  group_by(model_shorthand, n_params, condition, repeated) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_surprisal = mean(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, 
             y = mean_surprisal, 
             fill = repeated)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_surprisal - se, 
                    ymax = mean_surprisal + se),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(model_shorthand, n_params), nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("no" = "#B0B0B0", "yes" = "#4E79A7")) +
  labs(x = "Condition", 
       y = "Target token surprisal", 
       fill = "Repeated") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 14))

df_pythia_models %>%
  filter(checkpoint == 143000) %>%
  group_by(model_shorthand, n_params, condition, repeated) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_surprisal = mean(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(target_token_surprisal, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = repeated, 
             y = mean_surprisal, 
             color = condition,
             group = condition)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_surprisal - se, 
                    ymax = mean_surprisal + se),
                width = 0.15) +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(model_shorthand, n_params), nrow = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("dominant" = "#4E79A7", "subordinate" = "#E15759")) +
  labs(x = "Repeated", 
       y = "Mean target surprisal", 
       color = "Condition") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 14))
```


## By-model coefficients over training

```{r leinenger_coef}
## Set up contrast coding
df_pythia_models <- df_pythia_models %>%
  mutate(
    condition_c = ifelse(condition == "subordinate", 0.5, -0.5),
    repeated_c = ifelse(repeated == "yes", 0.5, -0.5)
  )


## Per-model coefficients with aligned LRT structure (contrast-coded)

dev_results_coefs <- df_pythia_models %>%
  group_by(model, model_shorthand, n_params, checkpoint) %>%
  group_map(~ {
    # Full interaction model
    full_model <- tryCatch(
      lmer(target_token_surprisal ~ condition_c * repeated_c +
             (1 | homophone), data = .x, REML = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    # Additive model (main effects only) - no interaction
    additive_model <- tryCatch(
      lmer(target_token_surprisal ~ condition_c + repeated_c +
             (1 | homophone), data = .x, REML = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    # Models for testing main effects (compared against additive_model)
    no_condition <- tryCatch(
      lmer(target_token_surprisal ~ repeated_c +
             (1 | homophone), data = .x, REML = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    no_repeated <- tryCatch(
      lmer(target_token_surprisal ~ condition_c +
             (1 | homophone), data = .x, REML = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    # LRTs - structure mirrors the pooled analysis
    lrt_interaction <- anova(additive_model, full_model)        # interaction: full vs additive
    lrt_condition   <- anova(no_condition, additive_model)       # main effect of Dominance
    lrt_repeated    <- anova(no_repeated, additive_model)        # main effect of Repetition
    
    # Extract coefficients and SEs from full model
    coef_summary <- summary(full_model)$coefficients
    
    tibble(
      model              = .y$model,
      model_shorthand    = .y$model_shorthand,
      n_params           = .y$n_params,
      checkpoint         = .y$checkpoint,
      
      condition_beta     = fixef(full_model)["condition_c"],
      condition_se       = coef_summary["condition_c", "Std. Error"],
      condition_chisq    = lrt_condition$Chisq[2],
      condition_p        = lrt_condition$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
      
      repeated_beta      = fixef(full_model)["repeated_c"],
      repeated_se        = coef_summary["repeated_c", "Std. Error"],
      repeated_chisq     = lrt_repeated$Chisq[2],
      repeated_p         = lrt_repeated$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
      
      interaction_beta   = fixef(full_model)["condition_c:repeated_c"],
      interaction_se     = coef_summary["condition_c:repeated_c", "Std. Error"],
      interaction_chisq  = lrt_interaction$Chisq[2],
      interaction_p      = lrt_interaction$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
    )
  }) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  arrange(n_params)


## Final-checkpoint analyses

dev_results_coefs_final <- dev_results_coefs %>%
  filter(checkpoint == 143000)

# Scaling: each effect against log params
cor.test(log10(dev_results_coefs_final$n_params),
         dev_results_coefs_final$condition_beta)
cor.test(log10(dev_results_coefs_final$n_params),
         dev_results_coefs_final$repeated_beta)
cor.test(log10(dev_results_coefs_final$n_params),
         dev_results_coefs_final$interaction_beta)


## Sign tests
binom.test(sum(dev_results_coefs_final$condition_beta > 0), n = 8)
binom.test(sum(dev_results_coefs_final$repeated_beta < 0), n = 8)
binom.test(sum(dev_results_coefs_final$interaction_beta < 0), n = 8)


## Pooled mixed-effects model (across all LMs at final checkpoint)

df_final <- df_pythia_models %>% filter(checkpoint == 143000)

# Full interaction model
pooled_full <- lmer(
  target_token_surprisal ~ condition_c * repeated_c +
    (1 | homophone) + (1 + condition_c | model_shorthand),
  data = df_final, REML = FALSE
)

# Additive model (no interaction)
pooled_additive <- lmer(
  target_token_surprisal ~ condition_c + repeated_c +
    (1 | homophone) + (1 + condition_c | model_shorthand),
  data = df_final, REML = FALSE
)

# No Dominance (compared to additive)
pooled_no_condition <- lmer(
  target_token_surprisal ~ repeated_c +
    (1 | homophone) + (1 + condition_c | model_shorthand),
  data = df_final, REML = FALSE
)

# No Repetition (compared to additive)
pooled_no_repeated <- lmer(
  target_token_surprisal ~ condition_c +
    (1 | homophone) + (1 + condition_c | model_shorthand),
  data = df_final, REML = FALSE
)

# LRTs - same structure as per-model
anova(pooled_additive, pooled_full)         # interaction
anova(pooled_no_condition, pooled_additive)  # main effect of Dominance
anova(pooled_no_repeated, pooled_additive)   # main effect of Repetition

summary(pooled_full)


## Developmental trajectory plots

# Dominance effect across training
dev_results_coefs %>%
  mutate(sig = condition_p < .05) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = checkpoint,
             y = condition_beta,
             color = reorder(model_shorthand, n_params))) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = .7) +
  geom_line(size = 1.3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Training Step (Log10)",
       y = "Coefficient (Dominance)",
       color = "") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1000, linetype = "dotted", size = 1.2) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "vertical",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm")) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = viridisLite::viridis(8, option = "magma",
                                                   begin = 0.8, end = 0.15))

# Interaction effect across training
dev_results_coefs %>%
  mutate(sig = interaction_p < .05) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = checkpoint,
             y = interaction_beta,
             color = reorder(model_shorthand, n_params))) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = .7) +
  geom_line(size = 1.3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Training Step (Log10)",
       y = "Coefficient (Repetition x Dominance)",
       color = "") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1000, linetype = "dotted", size = 1.2) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "vertical",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm")) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = viridisLite::viridis(8, option = "magma",
                                                   begin = 0.8, end = 0.15))


## LaTeX tables

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

format_p <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    p < 2e-16 ~ "<2e-16",
    p < 0.001 ~ formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2),
    TRUE ~ formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3)
  )
}

# Dominance table
table_dominance <- dev_results_coefs_final %>%
  arrange(n_params) %>%
  mutate(p_formatted = format_p(condition_p)) %>%
  select(
    Model = model_shorthand,
    `$\\beta$` = condition_beta,
    SE = condition_se,
    `$\\chi^2$` = condition_chisq,
    `$p$` = p_formatted
  )

kable(table_dominance,
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      digits = 3,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Dominance predicting target surprisal (final checkpoint).",
      label = "tables_dominance_leinenger") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"))


# Interaction table
table_interaction <- dev_results_coefs_final %>%
  arrange(n_params) %>%
  mutate(p_formatted = format_p(interaction_p)) %>%
  select(
    Model = model_shorthand,
    `$\\beta$` = interaction_beta,
    SE = interaction_se,
    `$\\chi^2$` = interaction_chisq,
    `$p$` = p_formatted
  )

kable(table_interaction,
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      digits = 3,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Repetition $\\times$ Dominance interaction predicting target surprisal (final checkpoint).",
      label = "tables_interaction") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"))
```
